title: Announce parse/format mismatch: Rust vs C for CCP-9 rx_channel in flags byte
description:
Rust/lichen-core/src/announce.rs (after commit 87c5112ee): FIXED_LENGTH=93; from_bytes does ; builder sets ; signed_data uses flags as channel. Matches test/vectors/ccp9.json vector 'announce_channel_parse_roundtrip' encoded starting '15010200..' (l2_dispatch=0x15, type=0x01, flags/channel=0x02). 

C in lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/announce.c:221:  then  (different offset), LICHEN_ANNOUNCE_MIN_LEN=94U, parse sets flags=data[1] expecting 0. build_signed_data puts rx_channel after seq_num.

Spec/05-routing.md:412-424 diagram shows 'Type=ANN | Flags | Hop Count | Seq Num | ...' with 'Flags: Reserved'; total ~92 bytes. No mention of rx_channel in flags or signed per CCP-9.

draft-lichen-link-01.md:107-112 mentions dispatch 0x15 for routing. 

Violates 'All implementations MUST produce identical output for test vectors' (README in test/vectors/), 'MUST match independent oracle in generate.py', and spec MUST/SHOULD for frame formats. Schnorr signing of rx_channel (to prevent tampering per 02a-coordinated-capacity.md: CCP-9) is broken in C.

See also lichen/subsys/lichen/link/include/lichen/l2_payload.h:33 LICHEN_L2_DISPATCH_ROUTING 0x15, LICHEN_L2_ROUTING_TYPE_ANNOUNCE 0x01.

Priority P0 interop bug.
close_reason: Aligned C parse/build/offsets/MIN_LEN=93 and Rust (resolved merge conflict) to match independent CCP-9 test vector oracle in generate.py and ccp9.json roundtrip (rx_channel in flags byte at offset 1 after type). Updated struct, tests, scheduler frame construction, signed data positions, offsets in parse/ingest. Fixes P0 interop and Schnorr signing of rx_channel.
